reason: The passage depicts Moses as the Hebrew leader who receives God's commands and directs the Israelites in the Passover observance. The text explicitly states that 'the children of Israel did according to the word of Moses' (וּבְנֵי־ יִשְׂרָאֵ֥ל עָשׂ֖וּ כִּדְבַ֣ר מֹשֶׁ֑ה). By ensuring the people follow these specific instructions—which are designed to protect them from the plague of the firstborn and facilitate their deliverance from Egypt—Moses actively takes responsibility for their welfare. He serves as the intermediary between God and the people, guaranteeing their adherence to the protective measures that preserve their lives.
